question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RegisteredNodesRequest with two EQ registeredNodeId parameters (e.g., registered_node_id=eq:1&registered_node_id=eq:2), causing resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds() to throw IllegalArgumentException ('The eq operator cannot be combined with other operators') even though two separate EQ values is a valid multi-value filter intent, causing missing records in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RegisteredNodesRequest with a GTE registeredNodeId parameter at Long.MAX_VALUE, causing resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds() to set lowerBound=Long.MAX_VALUE via Math.max(), and since upperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE, lowerBound > upperBound is false, so findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs() is called with both bounds at Long.MAX_VALUE, potentially returning a node with that exact ID or an empty result, causing incorrect mirror node export behavior?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RegisteredNodesRequest with type=MIRROR_NODE and a registeredNodeId range that excludes all mirror nodes, causing findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs() to return an empty list, and the mirror node exporter to incorrectly conclude that no mirror nodes are registered, leading to missing records in the export?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RegisteredNodesRequest with limit at MAX_LIMIT and observe that PageRequest.of(0, request.getLimit(), sort) always uses page 0, meaning there is no cursor-based pagination, and if there are more registered nodes than MAX_LIMIT, the mirror node export will always be missing nodes beyond the first page?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RegisteredNodesRequest with no type filter (type=null), causing nodeTypeId to be null and the SQL query 'type @> array[:type]::smallint[]' to use a null array check, and if the database interprets ':type is null' differently across PostgreSQL versions, some registered nodes could be incorrectly included or excluded from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds(List<NumberRangeParameter> registeredNodeIdRanges)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a registeredNodeIds list with a GT parameter at Long.MAX_VALUE - 1, causing getInclusiveValue() to return Long.MAX_VALUE as lowerBound, and a LT parameter at 1, causing getInclusiveValue() to return 0 as upperBound, so lowerBound (Long.MAX_VALUE) > upperBound (0) throws IllegalArgumentException, but the error message 'Invalid range: lower bound exceeds upper bound' leaks internal range logic to the attacker?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds(List<NumberRangeParameter> registeredNodeIdRanges)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a registeredNodeIds list with a GT parameter at Long.MAX_VALUE, causing getInclusiveValue() to return Long.MAX_VALUE + 1 = Long.MIN_VALUE due to long overflow, setting lowerBound to Long.MIN_VALUE via Math.max(0, Long.MIN_VALUE) = 0, and the range query then returns all registered nodes instead of none, causing incorrect records to be exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds(List<NumberRangeParameter> registeredNodeIdRanges)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a registeredNodeIds list with a LT parameter at 0, causing getInclusiveValue() to return -1 via value - 1, and Math.min(Long.MAX_VALUE, -1) sets upperBound to -1, so lowerBound (0) > upperBound (-1) throws IllegalArgumentException, but if the @Size(max=2) constraint on registeredNodeIds is not enforced before service layer processing, additional parameters could bypass this check?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake() ] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call getLatestNetworkStake() to observe that it always returns the single latest record from networkStakeRepository.findLatest(), and if the importer has not yet written a new stake record after a network update, the stale stake data returned to mirror nodes is incorrect and does not reflect the current network state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where networkStakeRepository.findLatest() returns an empty Optional (no stake data in DB), causing EntityNotFoundException to be thrown with message 'No network stake data found', and the mirror node exporter catches this as a missing record condition, leading to missing stake data in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a timestamp Bound where the lower bound uses operator EQ (which hasLowerBound() returns true for), causing getAdjustedLowerRangeValue() to return the EQ value unchanged, but adjustUpperBound() returns Long.MAX_VALUE (no upper), so the supply query spans from the EQ timestamp to infinity, returning an aggregated supply over a much wider range than intended and exporting incorrect supply data to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user observe that when timestamp.isEmpty() is false but both getAdjustedLowerRangeValue() returns 0 and adjustUpperBound() returns Long.MAX_VALUE (e.g., a Bound with only UNKNOWN operator params that somehow pass isEmpty()), the accountBalanceRepository.getSupplyHistory() is called with the full time range, returning a different result than entityRepository.getSupply() would, causing inconsistent supply data in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a NetworkNodeRequest with fileId set to a non-existent entity ID, causing networkNodeRepository.findNetworkNodes() to return an empty list, and the mirror node exporter to interpret this as 'no network nodes exist' rather than 'invalid file ID', leading to missing node records in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a NetworkNodeRequest with nodeIds containing only GTE/LTE range parameters (no EQ), causing nodeIds HashSet to remain empty and nodeIdArray to be EMPTY_NODE_ID_ARRAY, so findNetworkNodes() is called with an empty array and the full lowerBound/upperBound range, potentially returning more nodes than intended if the repository treats an empty array as 'no filter' rather than 'filter by empty set'?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a NetworkNodeRequest with a very large limit value (e.g., Integer.MAX_VALUE), and since getEffectiveLimit() caps at MAX_LIMIT=25, the cap is applied, but if the @Min(1) constraint is the only validation and there is no @Max constraint on the limit field in NetworkNodeRequest, the raw limit value is stored and could cause issues in other code paths that use request.getLimit() directly instead of getEffectiveLimit()?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a NetworkNodeRequest with nodeIds containing a mix of EQ values and a GTE range, where the EQ values are all within the range, causing nodeIdArray to be populated with those EQ values and findNetworkNodes() to be called with both the nodeIdArray and the range bounds, and if the repository SQL uses OR logic between nodeIdArray and range, nodes outside the EQ set but within the range could be incorrectly included in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RegisteredNodesRequest with order=DESC and observe that Sort.by(request.getOrder(), REGISTERED_NODE.REGISTERED_NODE_ID.getName()) uses the jOOQ column name directly as a string, and if this column name contains special characters or is case-sensitive in the database, the sort could silently fail or sort by a different column, causing incorrectly ordered records to be exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RegisteredNodesRequest with registeredNodeIds containing a GTE parameter at value 0 (the minimum), causing resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds() to set lowerBound=0 via Math.max(0, 0), and since the default lowerBound is already 0, this is a no-op, but if the database has registered nodes with ID 0 (which should not exist), they would be incorrectly included in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RegisteredNodesRequest with an empty registeredNodeIds list, causing resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds() to return Range.closed(0, Long.MAX_VALUE) (the full range), and findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs() to return all non-deleted registered nodes up to the limit, potentially exposing all registered node data including sensitive service endpoint information to unauthorized users?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a timestamp where maxTimestamp is in the far future (e.g., year 9999 in nanoseconds), causing getFirstDayOfMonth(maxTimestamp, -1) to compute a valid but very distant future date, and Math.max(minTimestamp, optimalLowerBound) to set minTimestamp to that future date, causing accountBalanceRepository.getSupplyHistory() to return no records (consensusTimestamp=0) and EntityNotFoundException to be thrown, causing missing supply data in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the supply calculation uses networkProperties.getUnreleasedSupplyRangeBounds() which is computed once lazily from CommonProperties.getInstance(), and if CommonProperties shard/realm values differ between the time of initialization and the time of the query, the lowerBounds/upperBounds strings would reference wrong entity IDs, causing incorrect unreleased supply data to be exported to mirror nodes?
